evidence: `src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts` keeps two public entry points (`reportRuntimeError` and legacy `handleError`) for the same responsibility, while packet context shows this file is a high-fan-in facade.

`src/integrations/supabase/client.ts` is also a compatibility alias surface (`getSupabaseRuntimeClient as getSupabaseClient`), reinforcing cross-codebase dependence on compatibility facades rather than a single canonical API.

`src/domains/generation/types/generationViewRow.ts` combines persisted row typing with derived display fields and UI optimistic overlays, and `src/domains/generation/types/shotViewRow.ts` composes on that mixed contract, blurring domain vs view ownership.

`src/domains/generation/hooks/useDeleteGenerationWithConfirm.tsx` imports `DeleteGenerationConfirmDialogState` from a UI dialog component type, so a domain hook depends on presentation-layer contract shape; `__tests__/useDeleteGenerationWithConfirm.test.ts` mirrors this coupling through dialog-centric assertions.

findings.summary: Compatibility alias facades create dual public APIs and prolong broad coupling.
evidence: `handleError.ts` exports both `reportRuntimeError` and legacy `handleError` for identical behavior.
`client.ts` re-exports runtime client under another stable alias (`getSupabaseClient`) rather than enforcing one canonical import path.
Both files are treated as high-import central facades in the review packet, so duplicated names amplify migration inertia.
findings.suggestion: Set a canonical API per facade and run a staged codemod migration: update callers to canonical names, add deprecation warnings/telemetry, then remove legacy aliases on a scheduled cutoff.

findings.summary: Domain type modules mix persistence, derived view fields, and UI runtime overlays.
evidence: `generationViewRow.ts` defines `GenerationRow` (persisted + derived display fields) and `GenerationUiRow` (adds optimistic UI overlay) in one domain types file.
`shotViewRow.ts` uses `GenerationRow[]` inside `ShotViewModel`, propagating display-layer assumptions into neighboring domain contracts.
findings.suggestion: Split type ownership into separate modules (`persisted`, `derived/view`, `ui-overlay`) and have feature/UI code import overlay types explicitly while domain-facing contracts depend only on persisted or derived domain-safe shapes.

findings.summary: Generation domain hook is coupled to dialog component contract.
evidence: `useDeleteGenerationWithConfirm.tsx` imports `DeleteGenerationConfirmDialogState` from a UI component module and returns that shape directly.
The test validates dialog-state fields (`dialogState.open`, `onOpenChange`, `onConfirm`) instead of a domain-level action contract, reinforcing UI-first coupling.
findings.suggestion: Define a domain-local confirm workflow contract (e.g., `ConfirmDeleteState`) in the hook/domain layer, adapt it to dialog props at the UI boundary component, and update tests to assert domain behavior rather than dialog prop shape.
